rulings.Case Title: MILENA MAIONCHI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison Motion For New Trial And For Code Of Civil Procedure 473 Relief Of Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 13, 2019, Line 2, Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison Motion For New Trial And For Code Of Civil Procedure 473 Relief Of Real Party In Interest Christopher Garrison Real Party in Interest Christopher Garrison's motion for new trial and for CCP 473 Relief is denied. Judge Quidachay has retired and is not available to decide this motion. Assuming without deciding that a real party in interest in a writ proceeding who didn't participate in the case prior to the decision on the writ has standing to move for a new trial or CCP 473 relief after the writ is decided, Mr. Garrison has not shown any of the grounds for a new trial or section 473 relief. Neither surprise at the outcome of the writ decision by Judge Quidachay nor reliance on the City to represent his interests is a cognizable ground for new trial or section 473 relief. Even if Mr. Garrison had shown a ground specified in either CCP 657 or 473, the motion would still have to be denied. Ironically, while Mr. Garrison complains of delay by petitioner Milena Maionchi, he fails to appreciate that he could have sought an earlier hearing date on the petition or opposed the petition on grounds of laches. In all events, Mr. Garrison has not provided any authority, nor is the court aware of any, that increased damages or monetary loss as a result of the pendency of a case is "prejudice" as that term is used in laches case law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Maionchi is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MILENA MAIONCHI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Milena Maionchi Motion To Strike Answer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 13, 2019, Line 2, Petitioner Milena Maionchi Motion To Strike Answer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification Petitioner Milena Maionchi's motion to strike answer to petition for writ of mandate is granted. The answer filed by real party in interest Christopher Garrison after the writ decision by Judge Quidachay is untimely and was not authorized by the court. Mr. Garrison has not cited any authority, nor is the court aware of any authority, allowing a real party in interest in a writ proceeding who didn't participate in the case prior to the decision to file an answer after the decision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Maionchi is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CHIKAKO IRIS HALL VS. GIGGLE, INC., DBA GIGGLE HEALTHY. HAPPY. BABY., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 13, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT SANFORD ROSEN Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Defendant Sanford Rosen's motion to stay this case pending the conclusion of proceedings in New York on the claims alleged by plaintiff Chikako Hall is granted. The Supreme Court of New York is the proper forum, at least in the first instance, for the adjudication of Ms. Hall's claims against Mr. Rosen. Mr. Rosen was appointed as an assignee per a New York statutory scheme and the New York court has supervisory authority over Mr. Rosen, which includes the authority to impose personal liability against Mr. Rosen for his conduct relating to his role as assignee. While California has an interest in upholding the enforcement of its laws and Ms. Hall is a California resident, principles of comity require that the New York court, not this court, adjudicate Ms. Hall's claims. There has been no showing that the New York court is either unable or unwilling to adjudicate those claims or will refuse to apply California law if determined that California law applies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Rosen is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MINOO PARSAZADEH VS. HAMPTON HEALTH, LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defefendant John Fullertons Attendance And Testimony At Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 13, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MINOO PARSAZADEH's Motion To Compel Defefendant John Fullertons Attendance And Testimony At Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Monetary Sanction Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JARED TAYLOR ET AL VS. TWITTER, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For For Attorney'S Fees Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 13, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF JARED TAYLOR, NEW CENTURY FOUNDATION Motion For For Attorney'S Fees Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 425.16 Plaintiffs Jared Taylor and New Century Foundation's motion for attorneys' fee per CCP 425.16 is denied. The unsuccessful arguments made by defendant Twitter, Inc. why the claims alleged by plaintiffs were not covered by the public interest exception to the activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute were neither totally and completely without merit nor solely intended to delay this case. Twitter's arguments fall well within the broad range of permissible and non-sanctionable arguments that a party may make. Although the court rejected those arguments, the arguments were based on reasoned analysis of the statutory text, the purpose of the public interest exception, and case law construing that exception. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Twitter is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The parties are informed that Judge Kahn expects to issue a decision on plaintiffs' motion for reconsideration by the end of this month. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SUE THOMPSON VS. MARY JANE FRANUS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Amended Motion And Amended Motion For Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Timothy H. Stearns; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 13, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT MARY FRANUS Amended Motion For Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Timothy H. Stearns; Proposed Order Defendant Mary Franus' amended motion for an award of mandatory attorney's fees and costs is granted in part. Ms. Franus is awarded $10,044.54 in fees and costs, the amount she sought in her initial motion. It is undisputed that, by her attorney, Ms. Franus agreed not to pursue further efforts to seek fees and costs for the services rendered in her successful anti-SLAPP motion in return for full payment of the amount she sought in her initial fees and costs motion. Plaintiff Jennifer Thompson fully performed her part of the agreement. The unilateral mistake of Ms. Franus' counsel is not a basis to rescind or otherwise not enforce the parties' agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for Ms. Thompson is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: RACHEL MOORE VS. PILOT AI LABS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 13, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANTs PILOT AI LABS, INC., JONATHAN SU and ROBERT ENGLISH's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Defendants Pilot AI Labs, Inc., Jonathan Su, and Robert English's motion to compel arbitration is granted in large part. This case is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings on all of the claims filed by plaintiff Rachel Moore other than her PAGA claim. Ms. Moore's PAGA claim is not subject to arbitration per the California Supreme Court's decision in Iskanian. Considered singly or collectively, none of the litigation conduct of the moving defendants constitutes a waiver of their right to arbitrate the claims of plaintiff Rachel Moore nor has Ms. Moore suffered prejudice from any of that conduct so that moving defendants have forfeited their contractual right to arbitration set forth in the March 24, 2017 offer of employment letter. A party's failure to allege an affirmative defense of arbitration or timely file an arbitration petition does not constitute either waiver or prejudice per the California Supreme Court's decision in St. Agnes Medical Center and case law since that decision. Moving defendants' propounding of discovery also does not constitute waiver or prejudice per St. Agnes and subsequent case law at least where, as here, there has been no showing that moving defendants gained information about Ms. Moore's case that they could not have learned about through arbitration. Moving defendants have not hid their desire to have Ms. Moore's claims arbitrated. What was said in Khalatian v. Prime Time Shuttle, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal. App. 4th 657, 663 has equal, if not greater, applicability to this case: "In Iskanian, our Supreme Court rejected the argument that a plaintiff's efforts over three years, including considerable effort and expense, resulted in waiver. Participating in litigation itself does not result in waiver. Causing the other party to incur expenses as part of litigation also does not result in waiver. (Here, there was no evidence that defendants stretched out the litigation process, gained information about plaintiff's case they could not have learned in an arbitration, or waited until the eve of trial to move to compel arbitration. Plaintiff identifies no information revealed during discovery that would not have been revealed in an arbitration...Because plaintiff demonstrated no prejudice from defendants' delay in moving to compel arbitration, the [trial] court erred in finding waiver." (Citations omitted). Ms. Moore has also not shown that there is anything substantively unconscionable about the parties' arbitration agreement. Absent substantive unconscionability, the parties' arbitration agreement is not unenforceable as unconscionable, even assuming there is procedural unconscionability. The carve-out for equitable relief is not substantively unconscionable per the reasoning of Baltazar v. Forever 21, Inc. (2016) 62 Cal.4th 1237 and Farrar v. Direct Commerce, Inc. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 1257. Contrary to Ms. Moore's arguments, the parties' agreement does not require Ms. Moore to pay more than she is required to pay per Armendariz nor does that agreement unreasonably limit her discovery rights. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
